mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) against rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 6.0% signals severe labor market deterioration that immediately triggers recession fears, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal's Sharpe ratio remains below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over 60 days in 70% of observed periods, or correlation between the interaction term and returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicate capacity constraints and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of late-cycle periods when INDPRO declines, or the mechanism's predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls confirm economic resilience, supporting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns show no directional bias following payroll increases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 220.0 force aggressive central bank tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL breaches 220.0, or the signal's out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized expansions in quarterly GDP and monthly retail sales confirm robust aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample tests, or forward returns show no significant positive skew.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contractions during a post-shock recovery phase signal fragile supply chains, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 50% of post-shock recovery periods when INDPRO declines, or the signal fails to achieve a minimum information coefficient of 0.04.
